2606.24371.title: Structural Kolmogorov-Arnold Convolutions: Learnable Function on the Values or the Filter Shape as Parameter-Efficient Alternative to Per-Edge Convolutional KANs
2606.24371.paper_summary: Convolutional Kolmogorov--Arnold Networks (KANs) replace the fixed weights of a convolutional kernel with learnable univariate functions. The dominant formulation attaches one such function to every kernel entry and lets it act on pixel values, expressive but parameter-heavy and prone to overfitting. We argue that the learnable functions are better placed in the \emph{structure} of the convolution than on each edge, and we organise the design space along a single axis: whether the function acts on the pixel \emph{values} or on the filter \emph{shape}. We study three realisations. SV-KAN applies one shared univariate function to the values and leaves the spatial filter free and static, aa classical convolution with a single learnable shared activation. AG-KAN keeps the shared value function but supplies the spatial structure through a content-adaptive Gaussian gate. RF-KAN instead moves the learnable functions onto the filter shape, building each filter from oriented ridge profiles expanded in a localised oscillatory (Morlet) wavelet basis with content-adaptive amplitudes. Under a matched four-layer protocol with in-run references and three seeds, RF-KAN and SV-KAN reach $88.47\pm0.10\%$ and $88.20\pm0.31\%$ on CIFAR-10 and $64.40\pm0.19\%$ and $64.57\pm0.30\%$ on CIFAR-100, at about $0.4$M parameters. At this matched scale the shape model and the simplest value model meet at the top, both above a plain convolution and every per-edge KAN we tested, including the official Gram variant, at roughly a fifth of the parameters. A controlled study attributes the RF-KAN gain to an intrinsically localised oscillatory basis and to content adaptivity, and an ablation that removes the learned shape entirely, leaving only the shared value function, collapses accuracy by over forty points, identifying the learned shape as the load-bearing ingredient at this scale.
2606.24371.paper_summary_zh: 卷積 Kolmogorov--Arnold 網路 (KANs) 用可學習的一元函數取代了卷積核的固定權重。主導的公式將這樣的函數附加到每個核條目上，並讓它作用於像素值，這種方式表達能力強但參數繁重且容易過擬合。我們主張可學習的函數應該更好地放置在卷積的\emph{結構}中，而不是在每個邊緣上，我們沿著單一軸組織設計空間：函數是作用於像素\emph{值}還是過濾器\emph{形狀}。我們研究了三種實現。SV-KAN 將一個共享的一元函數應用於值，並保持空間過濾器自由且靜態，這是一種具有單一可學習共享激活的經典卷積。AG-KAN 保持共享值函數，但通過內容自適應的高斯閘提供空間結構。RF-KAN 則將可學習的函數移到過濾器形狀上，從定向脊狀輪廓中構建每個過濾器，這些輪廓在局部振盪 (Morlet) 小波基上展開，並具有內容自適應的幅度。在一個匹配的四層協議下，使用運行中的參考和三個種子，RF-KAN 和 SV-KAN 在 CIFAR-10 上達到 $88.47\pm0.10\%$ 和 $88.20\pm0.31\%$，在 CIFAR-100 上達到 $64.40\pm0.19\%$ 和 $64.57\pm0.30\%$，參數約為 $0.4$M。在這個匹配的規模下，形狀模型和最簡單的值模型在頂部相遇，兩者都超過了普通卷積和我們測試的每個邊緣 KAN，包括官方的 Gram 變體，參數大約是其五分之一。一項受控研究將 RF-KAN 的增益歸因於固有的局部振盪基礎和內容自適應性，而一個去除學習形狀的消融實驗，僅保留共享值函數，準確率下降超過四十個點，確定學習形狀是這個規模下的承重成分。
2606.24371.author: Stefano Mereu et.al.
2606.24371.authors: Stefano Mereu, Oleksandr Kuznetsov, Gabriele Marchello, Alessandro Galdelli, Emanuele Frontoni, Adriano Mancini, Ferdinando Cannella
